source_jobs.title: Lead Credit Risk
source_jobs.description:
BUILD WITH US

Since 2004, we've had one clear goal: to make payments and money management effortless for every business in Europe.



Today, more than 250,000 companies use Mollie to get paid, manage money and grow – with products designed to be simple, scalable, and dependable.



With 950+ Mollies across 14+ locations, we care deeply about autonomy and craft. So we work in small teams, with real ownership, and we trust you to make the right decisions.

We're building for the long term, so we provide the tools you need, processes you can rely on, and a balanced work environment to help you do work you're proud of.



Sound like your kind of place? Let's build together.




YOUR IMPACT

Credit risk at Mollie isn't a back-office function — it's a core part of how we grow responsibly.

As Lead Credit Risk, you'll lead a team of five analysts in making sharp, well-reasoned decisions about merchant onboarding and portfolio health. You'll be the person who ensures we're approving the right merchants, at the right time, with the right level of exposure - and that our existing portfolio stays aligned with our risk appetite as it evolves.



But this role is about more than maintaining what's already working. Mollie is expanding and you'll be at the centre of that shift. You'll design and build the credit risk infrastructure - policies, models, processes - that makes it possible for us to confidently offer new financial products to our customers. That means building frameworks largely from scratch, in a fast-moving environment where your decisions have real and lasting impact.



You'll report directly to the Monitoring Operations Lead, and serve as the key point of contact on team performance, portfolio status, and strategic risk development. If you're energised by the idea of shaping something new - and you're hands-on enough to get into the details when it matters - this is the role for you.




WHAT YOU'LL SHIP

 - Full ownership of the existing merchant credit risk portfolio — hitting all team SLAs for underwriting and periodic reviews, and keeping portfolio quality sharp as merchant volumes grow.

 - A detailed, documented roadmap for the design and implementation of a new credit risk framework for Mollie's new products — built from the ground up.

 - A smart, scalable credit risk system that reduces friction in the customer lifecycle, enabling faster approvals for good merchants without compromising risk standards.

 - Clear performance and development plans for each member of the credit risk team, with a focus on coaching, growth, and building long-term capability.

 - Optimised assessment processes that leverage AI tooling to automate routine cases — freeing the team to focus human effort where it matters most.

 - Regular, structured reporting to leadership on portfolio performance, gaps, and where strategic attention is needed.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 5+ years of people management experience, with a track record of leading, coaching, and developing high-performing teams.

 - Hands-on experience in both PSP and banking environments, you understand how credit risk works differently across these contexts and can bridge the two.

 - Proven expertise in credit model and policy development, including building credit risk frameworks and scorecards from scratch.

 - Strong stakeholder management skills, you're comfortable working across sales, operations, and leadership, and you know how to communicate risk clearly to different audiences.

 - A hands-on, operational mindset, you don't just set direction, you roll up your sleeves and get into the details when the situation calls for it.

 - A genuine motivation to work in a fast-paced fintech environment and contribute to building new products , this role is for someone who's energised by growth, not slowed down by it.




GROW YOUR WAY

At Mollie, growth is personal. We believe everyone should have the chance to develop their skills, explore new challenges and shape their career

source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager - Romania
source_jobs.description:
Build with us

Businesses deserve better from finance. Less friction, more freedom. Since 2004, Mollie has been on a mission to make payments and money management effortless for every business in Europe.

Today, more than 250,000 companies trust our all-in-one platform to get paid, manage money and grow on their terms. Simple, scalable and built with real businesses in mind.

We're one of Europe's fastest-growing fintechs, with 950+ Mollies across 14+ locations. Our people make this growth possible. We move fast, build with purpose and care deeply about our customers. Whether you're solving problems, building market-leading products or exploring how AI can make work smarter, you'll have the freedom to create and the trust to deliver.

If you're ready to grow, shape the future of fintech and join an ambitious, high-performing team, this is the place for you.



Your impact

You will be the driving force behind Mollie’s expansion into the Romanian market. As our first dedicated Business Development Manager for Romania, you’ll have the unique opportunity to introduce our world-class payment solutions to a high-growth region. You aren't just selling a product; you are building Mollie’s reputation from the ground up, helping local SMEs and mid-market merchants ditch financial bureaucracy and scale faster.

This is a high-autonomy, "hunter" role where your local expertise and network will be your greatest assets. Working as part of our expanding CEE team, you’ll identify high-potential opportunities, navigate the local e-commerce landscape, and position Mollie as the preferred partner for ambitious Romanian businesses. If you thrive in entrepreneurial environments and want to own the success of an entire market, this is your lane.



What you'll ship

 - Identify and close new business opportunities within the Romanian SME and mid-market segments to drive rapid market share growth

 - Build and maintain a robust outbound pipeline through strategic prospecting, networking, and high-activity outreach

 - Pitch Mollie’s localised value proposition, focusing on our easy integration and superior checkout experience for Romanian consumers

 - Negotiate and close complex deals, managing the full sales cycle from initial discovery to go-live

 - Provide local market insights to our Product and Marketing teams to ensure our offering stays ahead of Romanian merchant needs

 - Maintain meticulous CRM records in Hubspot to ensure accurate forecasting and transparent pipeline management
   
   

What you'll bring

 - Professional fluency in Romanian and English is essential

 - A proven track record of exceeding targets in a B2B SaaS or fintech sales environment

 - Deep knowledge of the Romanian e-commerce ecosystem and local payment preferences

 - A self-starter "hunter" mentality with the discipline to build a territory from scratch

 - Excellent communication and negotiation skills with the ability to influence C-suite stakeholders

 - Strong technical aptitude to explain payment integrations and API benefits to prospective partners
   
   

Grow your way

At Mollie, growth is personal. We believe everyone should have the chance to develop their skills, explore new challenges and shape their career on their own terms.

You'll get regular feedback and performance reviews to support your development, with fair and transparent salary reviews along the way. Many Mollies move into new roles or take on new projects to stretch themselves, and we actively hire from within to help you take the next step.

No matter if you're aiming for promotion, exploring a different career path or building new skills, you'll be supported with the tools, trust and opportunities to grow your way.



Unlock your full potential and join us to eliminate financial bureaucracy

If you're excited by the idea of building what's next, for yourself and for thousands of businesses across Europe, we'd love to hear from you. Apply with your CV (in English) using the form below.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Application Engineer
source_jobs.description:
YOUR OPPORTUNITY

As an Enterprise Application Engineer, you possess extensive knowledge of administering and managing the most essential enterprise applications, including Google Workspace, GAM, JIRA, HiBob, Slack, Okta, Scripts, and more.

Your tech-savviness and expertise in working with APIs enable you to excel in building 'client-side' solutions. Thanks to your contributions, our current and new colleagues can work smoothly, driving the continued growth of Mollie.

To maintain our high level of support, we are seeking a talented individual to join our Enterprise Applications team and play a key role in managing and improving the Enterprise Applications fleet. By taking ownership of the Software/SaaS, Applications portfolio, you will ensure that Mollie always aims for the best solution.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING 

 - Elevate our Google Workspace environment to ensure seamless collaboration and productivity for our teams.

 - Managing and improving our Atlassian suite, enabling smooth project management and effective collaboration.

 - Administer, configure, and integrate our HRIS (HiBob) to ensure accurate data management, streamlined workflow processes, and seamless integration with internal platforms and external systems. 

 - Maintain comprehensive documentation of system configurations, processes, and integrations to support future scalability and compliance with organizational policies.

 - Plan, design, develop, and test software systems or applications, including cloud-based and internet-related tools, to enhance existing products and create new solutions.

 - Enable solutions that consider APIs, security, scalability, manageability, usability, and other critical factors to deliver complete and efficient solutions.

 - Identify opportunities to reduce technology spend and complexity, optimizing application usage.

 - Act as the escalation point and mentor for IT Engineers, resolving technical issues within the support portfolio.

 - Drive improvements, participate in projects, and continuously improve services to enhance efficiency and user experience.

 - Provide expert input in the design of strategic initiatives, focusing on the technology used and approaches to development and integration.

 - Ensure all applications and business automation platforms are reliable, resilient, secure, and compliant.

 - Collaborate with vendors to develop, install, and troubleshoot application systems, software, and development, enhancing the effectiveness of supported applications.





WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - A customer-first mindset, dedicated to meeting and exceeding user needs.

 - 4+ years of experience as a System Engineer or Enterprise Applications Engineer.

 - Advanced skills in managing Google Workspace, Okta, JIRA, Slack, GAM, Atlassian, HiBob and other industry-leading systems/platforms.

 - Understanding of Software/SaaS asset management and application lifecycle management principles.

 - Advanced technical troubleshooting abilities, ensuring efficient problem resolution.

 - Impeccable technical documentation and knowledge base writing skills.

 - Ability to work independently and with excellent organization, prioritizing a busy workload effectively.

 - Collaborative spirit, capable of working with cross-functional team members to meet customer needs.

 - Proactive in promoting technology innovation, researching and identifying relevant technologies.

 - Familiarity with installation and delivery of applications software using the development lifecycle.

 - Knowledge of Information Technology and Security-related practices to safeguard assets and data.

Your role is essential in fostering a deep understanding of our products and our customers' needs.

In this position, you'll work closely with the product and  commercial teams to ensure we develop a leading product narrative and identify opportunities to create awareness for the payments products. You'll lead go-to-market campaigns to launch new products, devise acquisition strategies, and ensure our teams are equipped to meet and exceed our targets.

 - Lead our product marketing efforts to drive the commercial success of our payments domain

 - Develop and execute the go-to-market plans for new products and features to enhance our proposition

 - Identify our ideal customer profile (ICP) and create key messaging by segment to drive new business acquisition.

 - Work with our partners to execute campaigns

source_jobs.title: Application Engineer II
source_jobs.description:
YOUR OPPORTUNITY



At Mollie, we’re on a mission to make payments and money management effortless for every business in Europe.

We started 20 years ago when we launched a more direct, affordable way for companies to get paid. That provided an alternative to the frustrating, overpriced solutions that banks offered at the time.

Today, we serve more than 250,000 businesses across Europe with an all-in-one solution that simplifies payments and money management. And we’re a 850-strong team of product, finance, support, commerce, and engineering specialists working across Europe – from Lisbon to London.



Our Lisbon hub:

Situated in the heart of Lisbon at Marques de Pombal, our team of 50+ engineers from 8 different nationalities, are all contributing to Mollie’s mission of delivering an outstanding Merchant Experience through our web and mobile app at the same time that we are scaling and enhancing our Payment Processing and Orchestration Platform This enables the launch of innovative Payments and Financial Products.

We take pride in our diverse, English-speaking culture that brings open minds to collaborate and solve financial challenges for our customers.



What you will be doing:

 - Building Large-Scale Systems: Help us design and engineer systems that can scale to manage high volumes of transactions efficiently and effectively.

 - API and Service Development: Design, build, and maintain APIs, services, and systems across Mollie’s engineering teams. 

 - Autonomy and Ownership: Lead technical initiatives from architecture design to implementation, ensuring engineering excellence throughout the process. 

 - Problem-Solving: Utilize your creative problem-solving skills and analytical abilities to resolve complex issues and contribute to our mission of making financial processes effortless.



What you will bring:

 - Always Be Shipping mindset: It’s about consistently delivering value to customers, where speed and quality go hand in hand.

 - 5+ years of experience in developing large-scale applications, preferably with PHP or Java. Experience with Go, C#, or Python is also valued but you are willing to work with PHP or Java.

 - Experience in designing systems and handling queuing, caching, concurrency, fault tolerance and scalability.

 - Excellent communication skills, with experience working effectively with various stakeholders. Creative problem-solving skills and the ability to shape ideas with a product-first approach, as well as analyse and resolve complex issues.







Kindly note that relocation support is not offered for this role.

source_jobs.title: Application Engineer II
source_jobs.description:
YOUR OPPORTUNITY 

Mollie is one of the fastest-growing Fintech in Europe, and we are facing many exciting technical and organisational challenges. Serving more than 250,000 businesses across the continent, we aim to cut through financial bureaucracy that hinders great ideas from growing. Our belief is that accepting payments, driving revenue, accessing capital, and managing money should be seamless for every business.



As an Application Engineer II at Mollie, you will have the opportunity to utilize your technical and leadership skills to develop innovative, user-friendly financial products and APIs that serve tens of thousands of merchants daily. Your role will focus on creating highly scalable solutions capable of processing numerous payments per second, where code quality and system performance are critical. You will play a vital part in building robust architectures and optimizing our systems to meet the increasing demands of our merchants, while also having the chance to bring your product development ideas to life as we pursue ambitious expansion plans.



Our Milan Hub:

In 2023, we successfully launched a new hub in one of the most vibrant cities in Europe: Milan! Since its inception, the Milan hub has been consolidated and continues to grow at a fast pace, contributing significantly to Mollie’s mission with multiple passionate agile engineering teams that will innovate, build, and enhance our portfolio of next-generation payment products. You'll work in a cross-functional team (Data, Machine Learning, Platform and Product) to accelerate our product delivery.



What you will be doing:



 - Technological Flexibility: We welcome experience in any object-oriented programming language. While we primarily use PHP and Java, we view these as tools to create solutions for our customers.

 - Building Large-Scale Systems: Help us design and develop systems that can scale to manage high volumes of transactions efficiently and effectively.

 - API and Service Development: Design, build, and maintain APIs, services, and systems across Mollie’s engineering teams. Work on service integrations with various systems to ensure seamless operations.

 - Collaboration Across Domains: Work closely with engineers, marketing, partnership, and product teams to build and enhance features on a large scale. Cross-functional collaboration is key to our success.

 - Communication: Excellent English communication skills, with experience working effectively with various stakeholders. Can explain complex problems and solutions in simple terms. Have the ability to influence technical and non-technical audiences

 - Autonomy and Ownership: Lead technical initiatives from architecture design to implementation, ensuring engineering excellence throughout the process. We value autonomy and the ability to drive projects independently.

 - Problem-Solving: Utilize your creative problem-solving skills and analytical abilities to resolve complex issues and contribute to our mission of making financial processes effortless.



What you will bring:



 - 3+ years of experience in Software Engineering

 - Experience with large-scale applications

 - Experience in designing systems and handling queuing, caching, concurrency, fault tolerance and scalability.

 - Always Be Shipping mindset



Kindly note that relocation support is not offered for candidates outside of Europe for this role.

Please apply to this job by sending over your CV in English.

source_jobs.description:

Your opportunity

As our new Business Development Manager you will be part of Mollie’s Sales team in Milan. We are looking to add skilled sales professionals who have an entrepreneurial mindset and are looking to get their hands dirty, have a data driven mindset and are eager to make a difference. Focusing on medium and large web shops with complex needs. This requires C-level and in-depth sales conversations.

 - You are a hunter and most of your experience is in outbound sales.

 - You feel confident discussing technical solutions.

 - You are organised, structured and analytical & you have a strong numbers and target focused approach.

 - You have strong networking skills and enjoy face to face interactions.

 - You are driven by impact and thrive in an entrepreneurial environment.

 - You are a strong relationship builder, you are transparent & honest and understand a consultative sales approach.

 - Experience in payments / fintech / SaaS is a plus.

 - Knowledge of e-commerce platforms like Prestashop, Magento and Shopify is a plus.

source_jobs.title: AML Compliance Officer - Belgium
source_jobs.description:
Build with us

Since 2004, we've had one clear goal: to make payments and money management effortless for every business in Europe.

Today, more than 250,000 companies use Mollie to get paid, manage money and grow – with products designed to be simple, scalable, and dependable.

With 950+ Mollies across 14+ locations, we care deeply about autonomy and craft. So we work in small teams, with real ownership, and we trust you to make the right decisions.

We're building for the long term, so we provide the tools you need, processes you can rely on, and a balanced work environment to help you do work you're proud of.

Sound like your kind of place? Let's build together.



Your impact

As Mollie's Anti-Money Laundering Compliance Officer (AMLCO) in Belgium, you will be a designated function holder with real regulatory authority. You'll own the design, oversight, and continuous improvement of Mollie's AML/CFT and sanctions framework for our Belgian operations, operating under a Dutch-passported EMI license. That means accountability for keeping us compliant with NBB and FSMA expectations, and managing all reporting obligations to the Financial Intelligence Processing Unit (CTIF-CFIFIU).

This role sits at the intersection of regulatory rigour and business ambition. Mollie is expanding its product portfolio in Belgium.. You'll bring hands-on financial crime expertise, take genuine regulatory ownership, and provide the local governance accountability that lets us scale with confidence.

Reporting to the Director of Compliance, you'll be part of a broader Compliance domain with room to grow. As the role evolves alongside Mollie's Belgian product expansion, so will your scope,making this an opportunity to build something meaningful.




What you'll ship

 - Own and continuously improve Mollie's AML/CFT and sanctions framework for Belgian operations, ensuring it stays fit for purpose as the business scales

 - Manage all regulatory reporting obligations to the CTIF-CFIFIU and maintain a strong, proactive relationship with Belgian supervisory authorities (NBB/FSMA)

 - Lead the local implementation of AML/CFT policies and controls in line with EU directives (AMLD, PSD2) and EBA Guidelines, translating regulatory requirements into practical, operational frameworks

 - Drive compliance readiness for new product launches in Belgium ensuring expansion happens on solid regulatory ground

 - Monitor the Belgian regulatory landscape and translate changes into timely, actionable updates to Mollie's compliance programme

 - Act as the internal point of expertise on Belgian financial crime compliance, advising stakeholders across the business and supporting a culture of compliance across teams



What you'll bring

 - 5–8+ years of experience in AML/CFT and financial crime compliance within a regulated financial institution — EMI, PSP, bank, or fintech

 - Prior experience as an AMLCO or deputy AMLCO, ideally within a Belgian regulatory context

 - Strong, working knowledge of the Belgian AML/CFT framework, EU directives (AMLD, PSD2), and EBA Guidelines — you know this space and can apply it practically

 - A track record of managing regulatory relationships and reporting obligations, including with bodies like the NBB, FSMA, or CTIF-CFIFIU

 - The ability to translate complex regulatory requirements into clear, actionable frameworks,without losing the detail and broader business context

 - Comfortable working across borders: this role involves occasional travel between Belgium and the Netherlands as part of a wider Compliance domain.

Credit risk at Mollie isn't a back-office function — it's a core part of how we grow responsibly.

As Senior Teamlead Credit Risk, you'll lead a team of five analysts in making sharp, well-reasoned decisions about merchant onboarding and portfolio health. You'll be the person who ensures we're approving the right merchants, at the right time, with the right level of exposure - and that our existing portfolio stays aligned with our risk appetite as it evolves.



But this role is about more than maintaining what's already working. Mollie is expanding and you'll be at the centre of that shift. You'll design and build the credit risk infrastructure - policies, models, processes - that makes it possible for us to confidently offer new financial products to our customers. That means building frameworks largely from scratch, in a fast-moving environment where your decisions have real and lasting impact.



You'll report directly to the Monitoring Operations Lead, and serve as the key point of contact on team performance, portfolio status, and strategic risk development. If you're energised by the idea of shaping something new - and you're hands-on enough to get into the details when it matters - this is the role for you.




WHAT YOU'LL SHIP

 - Full ownership of the existing merchant credit risk portfolio — hitting all team SLAs for underwriting and periodic reviews, and keeping portfolio quality sharp as merchant volumes grow.

 - A detailed, documented roadmap for the design and implementation of a new credit risk framework for Mollie's new products — built from the ground up.

 - A smart, scalable credit risk system that reduces friction in the customer lifecycle, enabling faster approvals for good merchants without compromising risk standards.

 - Clear performance and development plans for each member of the credit risk team, with a focus on coaching, growth, and building long-term capability.

 - Optimised assessment processes that leverage AI tooling to automate routine cases — freeing the team to focus human effort where it matters most.

 - Regular, structured reporting to leadership on portfolio performance, gaps, and where strategic attention is needed.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 5+ years of people management experience, with a track record of leading, coaching, and developing high-performing teams.

 - Hands-on experience in both PSP and banking environments, you understand how credit risk works differently across these contexts and can bridge the two.

 - Proven expertise in credit model and policy development, including building credit risk frameworks and scorecards from scratch.

 - Strong stakeholder management skills, you're comfortable working across sales, operations, and leadership, and you know how to communicate risk clearly to different audiences.

 - A hands-on, operational mindset, you don't just set direction, you roll up your sleeves and get into the details when the situation calls for it.

 - A genuine motivation to work in a fast-paced fintech environment and contribute to building new products , this role is for someone who's energised by growth, not slowed down by it.

Your impact

You will be the architect of Mollie’s success in the Czech Republic, acting as our first dedicated Business Development Manager for this high-potential market. We are a leader in Western Europe, but in the Czech Republic, we are the challenger. Your mission is to establish our brand, win over small and mid-market merchants, and prove that our unified API is the superior choice for local businesses looking to scale internationally.

This is a true "hunter" role where you will own your territory and strategy. Working as a vital member of our CEE Pod, you’ll navigate the unique nuances of the local e-commerce landscape—from local payment preferences to specific merchant needs. You’ll be the face of Mollie in the region, turning cold leads into long-term partners and setting the foundation for our long-term growth in the Czech market.



What you'll ship

 - Proactively identify and close new business opportunities within the Czech mid-market and SME segments to drive rapid market entry

 - Execute a high-velocity outbound sales strategy using cold calling, email sequencing, and social selling to build a sustainable pipeline

 - Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from initial discovery and stakeholder engagement to contract negotiation and go-live support

 - Present the technical and commercial value of Mollie’s omnichannel offering, focusing on localized payment methods and seamless API integration

 - Collaborate with Sales Engineers and Partner Managers to deliver tailored pitches that solve complex merchant pain points

 - Maintain rigorous CRM discipline to ensure data accuracy and transparent pipeline forecasting
   
   

What you'll bring

 - Native fluency in Czech and strong professional English are essential for this role

 - 3 to 5 years of commercial sales experience with a proven track record in B2B SaaS or fintech environments

 - A self-motivated "hunter" mindset with a relentless focus on outbound prospection and revenue growth

 - Deep understanding of the Czech e-commerce ecosystem, including local payment trends and competitor landscapes

 - Strong communication skills with the ability to articulate technical solutions to C-level stakeholders, IT managers, and finance leads

 - Experience using Salesforce or Hubspot to manage high-activity sales cycles and track performance metrics
